title: Purification of Proteins from PFA Fixed Samples with Biotin Pulldown for LC-MS/MS
author: Bryan Killinger
materials: Stop running, remove gel with clean gloves, and clean equipment.
Blot can now be probed with antibodies using standard protocols.
Briefly sonicate on low power to disperse tissue.
Carefully remove supernatant no to disturb beads.
Immediately place in -80 °C for long-term storage.
Spot 1 µL of each sample onto PVDF.

steps.title: Wash sections in DM 3 X 10 min
steps.instruction: Wash sections in DM 3 X 10 min.

steps.title: Place sections in 1
steps.instruction: Place sections in 1.5mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL of reversal buffer.

steps.title: Add 5 µL of 100 millimolar (mM) PMSF
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of 100 millimolar (mM) PMSF. Mix well. Quick spin.

steps.title: Heat on block for 98 °C...
steps.instruction: Heat on block for 98 °C for 00:30:00 with cap locks.

steps.title: Wash sections in DM for 00:10:00 (1/3)
steps.instruction: Wash sections in DM for 00:10:00 (1/3)

steps.title: Wash sections in DM for 00:10:00 (2/3)
steps.instruction: Wash sections in DM for 00:10:00 (2/3)

steps.title: Wash sections in DM for 00:10:00 (3/3)
steps.instruction: Wash sections in DM for 00:10:00 (3/3)

steps.title: Remove from heat block carefully (caps...
steps.instruction: Remove from heat block carefully (caps will pop if not careful).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 22000 x g, , for 00:30:00 Room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 22000 x g, , for 00:30:00 Room temperature .

steps.title: Collect S1 (extracted proteins)
steps.instruction: Collect S1 (extracted proteins).

steps.title: Perform methanol/chloroform cleanup on 100 µL of S1
steps.instruction: Perform methanol/chloroform cleanup on 100 µL of S1.

steps.title: Resuspend resulting pellet in 100 µL 5% SDS
steps.instruction: Resuspend resulting pellet in 100 µL 5% SDS.

steps.title: Perform BCA assay on 100 µL
steps.instruction: Perform BCA assay on 100 µL . Each BCA test takes 20 µL . The remaining volume can be used for western blot. Alternatively, more protein can be prepared from S1 if needed.

steps.title: Calculate volume required for 20 µg protein
steps.instruction: Calculate volume required for 20 µg protein.

steps.title: 20ug / concentration of S1 ug/ul...
steps.instruction: 20ug / concentration of S1 ug/ul = ul of sample required

steps.title: Add S1 to 10 mL TBST in 15mL conical tube
steps.instruction: Add S1 to 10 mL TBST in 15mL conical tube. Mix well.

steps.title: Add 40 µL of prepared magnetic...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL of prepared magnetic streptavidin beads to each tube.

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic stand for 00:01:00 (until all beads have been drawn out of solution).

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant no to disturb beads
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant no to disturb beads. Add 10 mL wash buffer.

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic stand for 00:01:00 (until all beads have been drawn out of solution).

steps.title: Add 10 mL wash buffer
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL wash buffer.

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic stand for 00:01:00 (until all beads have been drawn out of solution).

steps.title: Add 10 mL wash buffer
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL wash buffer

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic stand for 00:01:00 (until all beads have been drawn out of solution).

steps.title: Add 1 mL of wash buffer
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of wash buffer.

steps.title: Mix samples until beads are suspended in wash buffer
steps.instruction: Mix samples until beads are suspended in wash buffer.

steps.title: Using a pipette with tip cut...
steps.instruction: Using a pipette with tip cut off, transfer suspended beads to a 1.5mL protein low bind tube.

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic stand for 00:01:00 (until all beads have been drawn out of solution).

steps.title: Remove buffer and surface wash with milliq 2X
steps.instruction: Remove buffer and surface wash with milliq 2X.

steps.title: Quick spin and remove liquid from...
steps.instruction: Quick spin and remove liquid from tube (should only have beads left).

steps.title: Add 80 µL 1X SDS-page sample buffer containing reducing agent
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL 1X SDS-page sample buffer containing reducing agent.

steps.title: Mix well and quick spin
steps.instruction: Mix well and quick spin.

steps.title: Place on heat block set to 98 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Place on heat block set to 98 °C for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Mix well and quick spin
steps.instruction: Mix well and quick spin.

steps.title: Place on magnet
steps.instruction: Place on magnet.

steps.title: Transfer eluent to two separate lo-bind...
steps.instruction: Transfer eluent to two separate lo-bind tube and discard used beads. 35 µL in 1 tube (used for premass spec QC), the remaining 45 µL in another (used for mass spec).

steps.title: Prepare Bis-Tris 4-12% wedge gel 15 well
steps.instruction: Prepare Bis-Tris 4-12% wedge gel 15 well.

steps.title: Load 5 µL of MW standard...
steps.instruction: Load 5 µL of MW standard in one side of gel.

steps.title: For MS load exactly 40 µL...
steps.instruction: For MS load exactly 40 µL of the samples into wells. Careful not to spill into opposing wells. Only load samples into every other well to prevent cross contamination.

steps.title: Ensure all wells have a buffer
steps.instruction: Ensure all wells have a buffer. For empty wells fill with 40 µL 1X loading buffer.

steps.title: Run the gel at 150V for a few minutes
steps.instruction: Run the gel at 150V for a few minutes. Watch carefully until the sample has completely entered gel.

steps.title: Fix gel in 100mL fixation solution...
steps.instruction: Fix gel in 100mL fixation solution (refer materials section) for 01:00:00 Room temperature .

steps.title: Wash gel in several changes of...
steps.instruction: Wash gel in several changes of milliq, until gel has swollen to original size.

steps.title: Place in 100 mL colloidal Coomassie blue stain solution
steps.instruction: Place in 100 mL colloidal Coomassie blue stain solution. Cover loosely, and heat in microwave for few minutes until solution just starts to boil.

steps.title: Incubate gel in heated colloidal Coomassie...
steps.instruction: Incubate gel in heated colloidal Coomassie blue stain solution for 00:08:00 .

steps.title: Remove gel from stain solution and...
steps.instruction: Remove gel from stain solution and wash several times with milliq water. Clear background is achieved with ~ 02:00:00 of washing.

steps.title: Using a clean razor excise the...
steps.instruction: Using a clean razor excise the entire sample from the gel.

steps.title: Place each sample into a clean 1
steps.instruction: Place each sample into a clean 1.5mL tube. Add 500 µL of milliq water.

steps.title: Samples can be stored at 4...
steps.instruction: Samples can be stored at 4 °C until submitted to mass spec core.

steps.title: Estimating capture concentration - Purpose
steps.instruction: Estimating capture concentration - Purpose: To provide mass spec core with a rough estimate of the amount of protein in the capture sample. This prevents potential overloading, or digestion problems in the mass spec core.

steps.title: Prepare gel as described above (Day 4, Steps 43-45)
steps.instruction: Prepare gel as described above (Day 4, Steps 43-45)

steps.title: Load 20 µL of QC aliquot into wells
steps.instruction: Load 20 µL of QC aliquot into wells.

steps.title: Load 2 µg BSA into 1 well
steps.instruction: Load 2 µg BSA into 1 well.

steps.title: Run the gel at 150V for...
steps.instruction: Run the gel at 150V for approximately 00:45:00 or until the die has reached end of gel.

steps.title: Stain gel with Coomassie as described...
steps.instruction: Stain gel with Coomassie as described above (Day 4, Steps 50-54)

steps.title: Image on Odyssey using NIR
steps.instruction: Image on Odyssey using NIR.

steps.title: Forward image to NW mass spectrometry core
steps.instruction: Forward image to NW mass spectrometry core. Example of results; order = 2ug BSA, 3 captures, 2ug BSA, 3 captures, 2ug BSA, 3 captures

steps.title: Cut PVDF membrane into ~1X3 inch piece
steps.instruction: Cut PVDF membrane into ~1X3 inch piece.

steps.title: Activate PVDF in 100% methanol for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Activate PVDF in 100% methanol for 00:00:30

steps.title: Just prior to blotting samples, place...
steps.instruction: Just prior to blotting samples, place hydrated PVDF on wypall and quickly pat dry.

steps.title: Place PVDF on new dry wypall
steps.instruction: Place PVDF on new dry wypall.

steps.title: Allow PVDF to dry completely
steps.instruction: Allow PVDF to dry completely. (~1h or Overnight )

steps.title: Reactivate spotted PVDF as described above and equilibrate in dH20
steps.instruction: Reactivate spotted PVDF as described above and equilibrate in dH20.
